page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2ne.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 cores running at 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the lower tiers of modern cloud hardware. The Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor, while supporting instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512F, AES, and FMA, delivers a stress-ng single-core score of 1092.58 and a multi-core score of 1442.69, both falling in the bottom 25% and 10% respectively. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with read-only large block transfers measured at 10.65 GB/sec and write speeds at 9.53 GB/sec.

From a workload perspective, the ecs.sn2ne.large is suited for lightweight, non-intensive tasks. It achieves a static web serving throughput of 1.26 GB/sec and can process 1.43 million Redis SET operations per second. It is appropriate for basic web serving, development environments, and small-scale database operations where high-throughput computational performance is not a primary requirement.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2ne.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor at 2.5 GHz. Utilizing the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture, this server provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio but lacks local storage and GPU acceleration. Benchmark results indicate entry-level performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1092.58 and a multi-core score of 1442.69, placing it in the lower performance tiers. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. This instance is best suited for lightweight, non-compute-intensive workloads such as basic web serving, small database operations, and development environments.

tagline: A dedicated dual-core virtual server with balanced memory allocation for lightweight, entry-level hosting and development workloads.

8.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for basic web serving, development environments, and light database tasks
